topics.title: Europe stays on heat alert
topics.summary: A severe heatwave is keeping much of Europe on alert, breaking June records, closing schools, disrupting transport and pushing health services to prepare for more heat-related emergencies.
topics.discussionPrompt: When weather starts changing plans, do you try to adapt and carry on, or do you prefer to cancel everything and hide indoors?

topics.title: Scientists link Europe heat to climate change
topics.summary: World Weather Attribution scientists say Europe's record June heatwave would have been virtually impossible without human-caused climate change, with extreme warm nights now far more likely.
topics.discussionPrompt: Do scientific explanations make extreme weather easier to understand, or does it still feel too big and abstract to connect to daily life?

topics.title: Canada's TSX opens lower
topics.summary: Canada's main stock index opened slightly lower Friday, as weakness in technology shares and softer oil prices offset some optimism around artificial intelligence demand.
topics.discussionPrompt: It is funny how AI news can lift some companies while the wider market still slips. Do stock moves ever feel logical to you?

topics.title: World Cup sets goal record
topics.summary: The 2026 World Cup has broken the tournament record for most goals, passing the old mark of 172 after Auston Trusty scored for the United States against Turkey.
topics.discussionPrompt: A high-scoring tournament sounds fun for casual fans. Do you prefer lots of goals, or a tense low-scoring match with everything on the line?

topics.title: Canada wholesale trade likely dipped
topics.summary: Statistics Canada's flash estimate says wholesale trade likely fell 0.7% in May, mainly because of lower sales in machinery, equipment and supplies.
topics.discussionPrompt: Those behind-the-scenes numbers can hint at where the economy is headed. Do you find business statistics useful, or mostly just background noise?
